steelman_no_trade.draft: A rational Senior could pass because the market may already price in durable platform growth, while regulatory, insurance, and labor risks can pressure margins abruptly.
steelman_no_trade.source_label: C-4 DCF scenarios and UBER filed spine
steelman_no_trade.excerpt_or_summary: The filed DCF scenario set and spine identify a profitable platform with valuation sensitivity to growth, margins, WACC, and regulatory costs.

counterparty.draft: The plausible counterparty is a growth holder reducing exposure because the stock already discounts sustained platform leverage while labor, insurance, and local competition risks remain hard to time.
counterparty.source_label: C-1 Business and C-4 DCF scenarios
counterparty.excerpt_or_summary: Business and scenario artifacts identify regulatory and margin durability as the central disagreement mechanism.

draft.mechanism: Investors can underweight the durability of local network density and advertising or membership attach because reported results mix Mobility, Delivery, and Freight cycles.
draft.persistence_reason: The disagreement can persist until several reporting periods show whether gross bookings growth converts into stable incremental margins.

structural_mispricing.source_label: C-2 Moat and C-4 DCF scenarios
structural_mispricing.excerpt_or_summary: Moat and scenario drafts support marketplace density and operating leverage as the main variant-view drivers.

variant_view.draft: The variant view is that the market is underweighting marketplace density, advertising and membership attach, and operating leverage while over-indexing on regulatory and insurance headlines.
variant_view.source_label: C-1 Business, C-2 Moat, and C-4 DCF scenarios
variant_view.excerpt_or_summary: Filed Analyst artifacts support a profitable platform DCF route rather than an inventory or asset-turnover thesis.

draft.event: Quarterly gross bookings, trips, and adjusted operating profit disclosures
draft.why_it_matters: Tests whether marketplace growth is converting into durable profit.

draft.event: Major labor, insurance, or local-market regulatory updates
draft.timing: ongoing through the next annual filing cycle
draft.why_it_matters: Tests whether legal and insurance risks absorb operating leverage.

catalysts.source_label: C-4 DCF scenarios and C-6 risk setup
catalysts.excerpt_or_summary: Scenario and risk artifacts identify growth conversion, margin durability, and regulatory cost as thesis drivers.

cruxes.claim: Gross bookings growth must keep converting into operating profit.
cruxes.threshold_value: growth remains positive while operating margin is stable to improving

cruxes.claim: Regulatory and insurance costs must not absorb platform leverage.
cruxes.threshold_value: cost growth that offsets most incremental operating profit

cruxes.claim: The platform must avoid renewed incentive-heavy growth.
cruxes.threshold_value: sustained incentive escalation that compresses take-rate economics

source_evidence_summary.business: Business artifact frames UBER as a multi-sided mobility, delivery, and freight marketplace.
source_evidence_summary.moat: Moat artifact supports network density and marketplace liquidity while flagging regulation and incentive intensity.
source_evidence_summary.capalloc: Capital allocation artifact supplies reinvestment, dilution, and balance-sheet flexibility context.
source_evidence_summary.scenarios: Scenario artifact identifies DCF-specific growth, margin, and WACC drivers.
source_evidence_summary.mechanical: Gate card, method directive, DCF valuation, reverse DCF, and spine provide mechanical context.
